title: Git Sync: Manage Dashboards as Code

blocks.content:
Git Sync connects a GitHub repository to your Grafana instance so that dashboards are automatically stored and versioned as code. Edit a dashboard in Grafana and the change is pushed to Git; commit a change in Git and Grafana pulls it in.

In this guide you will explore an active Git Sync connection, create a dashboard that syncs to a repository, and clean it up afterward.
You'll open the Provisioning settings and inspect the health, resources, and pull status of an existing Git Sync connection.
Open **Provisioning** from the main menu.
blocks.tooltip: Provisioning manages external sources of Grafana resources like Git repositories.

blocks.content: Click **View** to open the connected repository.
blocks.tooltip: Each connected repository shows its sync status, managed resources, and recent jobs.

blocks.content: Review the **Resources** card. It lists the types and counts of Grafana resources managed by this repository, such as folders and dashboards.
blocks.tooltip: This card updates as you add or remove resources from the synced folder.

blocks.content: Check the **Health** card. It reports whether Grafana can reach and authenticate with the remote repository.
blocks.tooltip: A healthy status confirms the Git connection and credentials are working.

blocks.content: Check the **Pull status** card. It shows the result of the last sync, the most recent commit reference, and when the last successful pull occurred.
blocks.tooltip: Grafana pulls changes from the repository on a schedule or when a webhook fires.

blocks.content: Switch to the **Resources** tab to see every individual resource managed by this repository.
blocks.tooltip: Each row shows a resource's title, type, current sync status, and commit hash.

blocks.content: Click **Pull** to manually trigger a sync from the remote repository.
blocks.tooltip: Use this to pull changes immediately instead of waiting for the next scheduled sync.

blocks.content: You've explored the provisioning overview for a Git Sync repository, including its health, resources, and pull status.

blocks.title: Add a dashboard to the synced folder

blocks.content: You'll create a dashboard from a template and save it into the Git Sync folder so it is automatically committed and pushed to the connected repository.
Navigate to the synced dashboards folder and create a new dashboard from a template.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid browse-dashboards-table'] div[data-testid='data-testid browse dashboards row git-sync-dashboards/']

steps.description: Click **New** to open the create menu.

blocks.content: Save the new dashboard into the **git-sync-dashboards** folder. Because this folder is managed by Git Sync, the dashboard JSON is automatically committed and pushed to the repository.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid dashboard controls'] button[data-testid='data-testid Save dashboard button']
steps.tooltip: Opens the save dialog where you choose a title and folder.
Opens the folder picker so you can choose where to store the dashboard.
Saves the dashboard and triggers a Git Sync push to the repository.

blocks.content: Open the GitHub repository to verify your new dashboard has been synced. Once you see the dashboard file on the **main** branch, return to this guide.
blocks.tooltip: Git Sync pushes the dashboard JSON to the repository automatically.

blocks.content: You've created a dashboard and confirmed it was automatically pushed to the connected Git repository.
You'll delete the dashboard you just created to confirm that deletions also sync back to the Git repository.
Click the **git-sync-dashboards/** breadcrumb to return to the folder.
blocks.tooltip: Breadcrumbs let you navigate back up the folder hierarchy.

blocks.content: Select the dashboard you created by clicking its checkbox.
blocks.tooltip: Selecting items enables bulk actions like moving or deleting.

blocks.content: Delete the selected dashboard and close the confirmation drawer.

steps.tooltip: Opens a confirmation dialog before deleting.
Confirms the deletion and triggers a Git Sync commit removing the file.
Closes the results drawer to return to the folder view.

blocks.content: You've deleted the dashboard. Git Sync will commit the removal to the repository, keeping Grafana and Git in sync.

**Congratulations!** You've completed the full Git Sync workflow: inspecting a connected repository, creating a dashboard that automatically syncs to Git, and confirming that deletions flow back to the repository. Any dashboard saved into a Git Sync folder is version-controlled without leaving the Grafana UI.
